note: worktree was dirty at task creation

Decision: changes_requested (revision 1). Claimed suites pass, but direct source and temp-DB probes found contract blockers. 1) The public PortraitSource seam has close() and public index exports direct adapters/schema gate, despite the approved external interface having only four methods; capability notes also expose v_workpls_dimension_evidence. Keep lifecycle/disposal outside the four-method consumer seam and remove view/table leakage. 2) Resolver treats a missing data_source table as an absent row and defaults to pls_workspace. Missing required schema is not a missing config row; fail closed. 3) plsWorkspaceDbPath is optional in resolver/AgentHarness construction, so omitting it accepted the same DB as AgentHarness. Make self-DB comparison non-bypassable and test omission. 4) schema gate uses pragma_table_info only and accepted three same-named tables as required views. Verify sqlite_master type=view and reject table masquerading. 5) duplicate snapshotId rows were returned and resolve used LIMIT 1, silently choosing v1 from v1/v2. Reject ambiguous object/snapshot identities for both adapters; PLS discovery must also filter the approved comparable profile_stage and reject blank source_batch_id. 6) resolve selects too few evidence fields and accepted dataVersion=WRONG, profile_time_window from another period, source_batch_id=WRONG_BATCH, evidence-ref batch mismatch, and metric_aggregation=avg. Select and validate all binding fields against the resolved snapshot and approved 0.3.0 row contract, reject duplicate dimension/metric ambiguity, and add negative tests. 7) normalizeToUtcMs accepted date-only 2026-01-01 and manufactured a UTC millisecond timestamp. Accept only approved UTC Z timestamp formats and reject date-only/offset/local forms. Independent validation: typecheck passed; portrait-source 44/44 passed outside sandbox after tsx IPC EPERM; algorithm 15/15; schema 30/30; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths passed. These passing tests omit the demonstrated boundaries. Memory Used: Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts affected comparison with real PLS schema and AgentHarness migrations; External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary affected the row/schema/identity probes; Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence affected matching claimed coverage to the 44 real tests. Memory metadata updated to use_count 22, 4, and 10 respectively, with 2026-07-19 last use and 2026-10-17 expiry.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 2). Revision 1 fixed the prior seven blocker groups, and typecheck plus portrait-source 56/56, algorithm 15/15, and schema 30/30 pass (portrait-source rerun outside sandbox because tsx IPC hit EPERM). Actual source/negative review still finds bounded contract gaps: 1) The brief requires AgentHarness evidence capability to be construction-gated by real non-mock, unit-bearing evidence. createAhDb() has zero evidence, yet the test explicitly expects readiness=ready/evidenceResolutionAvailable=true; resolvePortraitSnapshot also returns dimensionEvidence=[] instead of PortraitSourceNotReadyError. Compute and store workspace evidence availability once at construction, report not_ready when absent, and fail closed on resolve. 2) Full AgentHarness 0.3.0 binding remains incomplete: select/validate profile_id against snapshot_id; require source_quality_flags_json to equal the resolved snapshot quality flags; require every evidence ref sourceRecordType exactly platform_profile_tag_metric (not merely non-blank); add negative tests. 3) normalizeToUtcMs still accepts semantically invalid timestamps such as 2026-02-29T00:00:00Z and 2026-01-01T24:00:00Z because Date.parse normalizes them. Reject invalid calendar/time components rather than manufacturing another instant. 4) Normal AgentHarness close() still swallows db.close() errors despite the brief requiring explicit error handling and lifecycle proof; surface a controlled adapter error while keeping constructor cleanup best-effort where preserving the original failure is appropriate. Keep W05 blocked. Memory Used: "Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts" affected comparison with the real AgentHarness 0.3.0 docs/validation; "External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary" affected the empty-evidence capability and full-binding review; "Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission" affected inspection of the 56 tests and exposed the incorrect empty-source ready expectation. Metadata updated to use_count 23, 5, and 11, last_used_at 2026-07-19, expires_at 2026-10-17.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 3). The declared suites pass: typecheck, portrait-source 63/63, algorithm 15/15, schema 30/30; tsx suites were rerun outside sandbox after IPC EPERM. Revision 2 closes the empty-workspace readiness, quality-flag, exact sourceRecordType, calendar validation, and normal close-error reporting cases, but actual source review still finds bounded blockers: 1) The previous review explicitly required profile_id binding, but resolve still does not SELECT or validate profile_id == snapshot_id; handoff claims all four blockers closed while omitting this requested check. Add the field and negative test. 2) hasEvidence is workspace-global. If any other snapshot has evidence, resolving a snapshot with zero rows still returns dimensionEvidence=[] instead of failing closed. Require non-empty evidence for the requested object/snapshot and test the mixed workspace case. 3) normalizeToUtcMs discards valid input milliseconds: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.mmmZ is accepted but Date.UTC is called without milliseconds, manufacturing .000Z. Preserve the exact millisecond component and test non-zero milliseconds. 4) Construction evidence probing catches every SQLite/query error and silently converts it to hasEvidence=false. Query failure is source unavailable/contract failure, not absence; close and throw a controlled adapter error. 5) close() marks closed before db.close(); when close fails, a retry becomes a no-op and may leak the connection. Only finalize the closed state after successful close, or otherwise preserve a safe retryable/terminal lifecycle with a regression seam. 6) docs/notes-backend.md remains stale at 44/44 and says waiting review even though the handoff claims it was updated to current W04 facts; update the allowed notes section to the actual 63-test revision state. Keep W05 blocked. Memory Used: "Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts" affected checking the omitted profile_id claim against source; "External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary" affected snapshot-specific evidence, probe-error, timestamp-fact, and close-lifecycle decisions; "Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission" affected matching handoff claims to the 63 actual tests and stale notes. Metadata updated to use_count 24, 6, and 12, last_used_at 2026-07-19, expires_at 2026-10-17. Memory Candidates were reviewed but not promoted during this task review.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 4). Typecheck and the declared suites pass: portrait-source 66/66, algorithm 15/15, schema 30/30; tsx reran outside sandbox after IPC EPERM. The revision 3 implementation adds the requested branches, but proof and controlled-error requirements remain incomplete: 1) The test named "evidence profile_id mismatch" does not exercise the new profile_id check. It inserts snapshot_id='profile_other' and resolves snapshotId='profile_001', so the query returns zero rows and the test passes at the snapshot-specific empty-evidence guard. Keep snapshot_id='profile_001' while setting profile_id='profile_other', and assert the profile binding error path. 2) No regression test exercises evidence-probe query failure, despite the handoff claiming this blocker closed. Add a schema-compatible view/probe failure fixture and assert construction throws the controlled unavailable error, closes the connection, and does not report not_ready. 3) No test exercises db.close() failure/retry, although the brief requires connection lifecycle proof and the previous review requested a regression seam. Add an injectable/owned close seam or equivalent bounded test proving the first controlled failure leaves release retryable and the subsequent successful close reaches the closed state. 4) Probe and close errors currently interpolate raw SQLite/provider error.message into public PortraitSourceUnavailableError text, contrary to the brief's prohibition on passing raw provider errors upward. Use stable controlled public messages; preserve the original only as internal cause if needed. 5) docs/notes-backend.md still says 63/63 in the heading, progress, and test-count line; the handoff explicitly claims it was updated to the actual 66/66 state. Correct all three facts. Keep W05 blocked. Memory Used: "Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts" affected comparison of the claimed profile test and notes against actual files; "External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary" affected probe/close lifecycle and provider-error decisions; "Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission" exposed that the named profile test passes through the wrong branch and two claimed boundaries have no tests. Metadata updated to use_count 25, 7, and 13, last_used_at 2026-07-19, expires_at 2026-10-17. Memory Candidates reviewed but not promoted.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 5). Typecheck and suites pass: portrait-source 66/66, algorithm 15/15, schema 30/30; tsx reran outside sandbox after IPC EPERM. The profile_id test now reaches the intended row, stable public messages are fixed, and notes show 66/66. However the worker explicitly declined the two lifecycle regression tests required by the prior review and by the brief's "连接关闭/无泄漏风险" evidence gate. Difficulty mocking SQLite is not a waiver, and an internal test seam is within the existing allowed adapter/test paths without changing the four-method public seam. Required bounded revision: 1) Extract/inject an internal evidence-probe operation (or equivalent minimal DB seam) so a deterministic thrown provider error proves construction closes the owned connection and throws PortraitSourceUnavailableError with the stable message rather than returning not_ready. 2) Extract/inject the internal owned-resource close operation (or equivalent) so a first close failure proves a controlled stable error and retryable state, then a second success proves closed/idempotent behavior. Keep these hooks internal to agentharness-adapter.ts/direct contract-test imports; do not export them through portrait-source/index.ts or broaden durable config. 3) Assert exact stable public error messages and absence of injected provider text. Existing schema-drift and happy close tests do not execute these catch/retry branches and cannot substitute for them. No validation waiver is granted. Keep W05 blocked. Memory Used: "Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts" affected rejecting the unsupported "beyond allowed paths" claim after inspecting allowed paths; "External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary" affected requiring deterministic probe cleanup and retryable close proof; "Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission" affected confirming the two claimed paths remain absent from the 66 tests. Decision: changes_requested (revision 6). Typecheck and suites pass: portrait-source 69/69, algorithm 15/15, schema 30/30; tsx reran outside sandbox after IPC EPERM. Internal seams and stable-message assertions were added, but the lifecycle tests still do not prove resource release: 1) On probe failure, production catch calls db.close() directly and never calls injected _closeDb. The test's proof—opening the same SQLite file again—does not show the original connection closed because SQLite permits concurrent read-only connections. Route all owned-connection cleanup through one internal close seam and assert the injected cleanup received the exact opened DatabaseSync and ran once before the controlled error. 2) In the close retry test, the second injected "success" is a no-op; it does not call _db.close(), yet the adapter marks itself closed. The real connection is leaked while the test only proves a state flag. On the second attempt call the supplied DatabaseSync.close() (or use a fake owned resource whose released state is observable), then assert actual release plus closed/idempotent behavior. 3) The stable-message test injects an always-failing close and never releases the real connection in finally; ensure tests close owned DBs even after intentional failures. Keep the seams internal and public four-method interface unchanged. Existing 69/69 is therefore not sufficient for the brief's connection-close/no-leak gate. Keep W05 blocked. Memory Used: "Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts" affected rejecting the handoff's reopen=no-leak claim against SQLite connection semantics; "External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary" affected requiring the same owned-resource close seam on constructor cleanup and retry; "Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission" affected tracing what the three new tests actually execute rather than trusting their names. Metadata updated to use_count 27, 9, and 15, last_used_at 2026-07-19, expires_at 2026-10-17. Memory Candidates reviewed but not promoted.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 7). Typecheck and suites pass: portrait-source 69/69, algorithm 15/15, schema 30/30; tsx reran outside sandbox after IPC EPERM. The normal close retry now calls the real DatabaseSync.close(), and stable-message cleanup retries release the DB. Remaining bounded blockers: 1) The probe lifecycle test still does not inject _closeDb or assert cleanup received the same DatabaseSync used by _probeEvidence; it still relies on reopening the file, which does not prove the original connection was closed. Capture the db argument in _probeEvidence, inject _closeDb that records/asserts strict identity, calls db.close(), and assert exactly one cleanup before the controlled exception. 2) The handoff claims ALL constructor cleanup routes through the seam, but post-open operations are not under a unified ownership guard: db.prepare("PRAGMA query_only"), verifyStmt.get(), and validateViewSchema(db) can throw and bypass cleanupDb entirely. Wrap the complete post-open initialization in a single ownership-transfer try/catch (or equivalent finally guard): every exception before successful adapter construction must call cleanupDb exactly once, while successful construction transfers ownership to the adapter. Preserve existing controlled error taxonomy without double-close. Add at least one injected failure before probe (query-only verification or schema gate seam) proving cleanup. 3) docs/notes-backend.md heading says revision 6 but its main T0039 progress line still says revision 5; make the status internally consistent. Existing 69/69 therefore does not yet satisfy the explicit "构造中途失败需关闭已打开连接" gate. Keep W05 blocked. Memory Used: "Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts" affected checking the handoff's ALL-path claim against unguarded prepare/schema calls and stale notes; "External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary" affected requiring a single ownership-transfer guard; "Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission" affected confirming probe cleanup identity is still not asserted. Metadata updated to use_count 28, 10, and 16, last_used_at 2026-07-19, expires_at 2026-10-17. Memory Candidates reviewed but not promoted.

Decision: approved. Revision 7 closes the final lifecycle blockers: all post-open AgentHarness initialization is under one ownership-transfer guard; probe failure cleanup uses the same injected close seam and asserts exact DatabaseSync identity plus single invocation; schema-gate failure cleanup is covered; close failure retries call the real db.close(); stable public errors exclude provider text; public four-method seam remains unchanged; notes consistently report revision 7 and 70/70. Independent validation: server typecheck passed; portrait-source 70/70, algorithm 15/15, schema 30/30 passed outside sandbox after tsx IPC EPERM; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths passed. No validation waiver. Out-of-scope dirty paths are pre-existing approved batch/controller changes and are explicitly accepted for Task Bus state transition only; they are not attributed to T0039. Memory Used: "Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts" affected source-vs-handoff inspection; "External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary" affected final ownership and cleanup acceptance; "Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission" affected direct verification of all 70 tests and lifecycle branches.
